apiChanges.change: Add framing and density props for default, embedded, and alternate-frame treatments plus a dedicated empty-state surface.
apiChanges.reason: Current usage assumes one panel style and forces docs/mock contexts to compensate outside the component.

apiChanges.change: Replace the narrow filename/fileMeta row with reusable file metadata regions and configurable frame chrome.
apiChanges.reason: Chat and metadata-heavy contexts need more than one extra string and currently cannot express richer file state cleanly.

apiChanges.change: Add frame variant support, asymmetric layout control, and shared metadata/count surfaces.
apiChanges.reason: One-sided diffs currently render as two columns with placeholder empties, and mock/docs framing still lives outside the component.

apiChanges.change: Support side visibility or mode controls beyond implicit before/after presence.
apiChanges.reason: Known added-only, removed-only, and emphasis-first states need deliberate one-sided composition rather than incidental empty panels.

implementationPlan: Expand the shared Code component types and markup so framing, metadata regions, and asymmetric diff behavior are first-class APIs.
Update component CSS to support embedded and alternate-frame treatments while staying theme-aware in vellum and void contexts.
Replace recurring mock/chat/docs wrappers where the pattern is clearly reusable, especially the Seraph diff wrapper and dense editor metadata treatments.
Rewrite/add stories so metadata-heavy, embedded, alternate-frame, one-sided, and empty states are direct component stories.
Strengthen tests for the new rendering permutations before running the final validation sweep.

recurringWrappers.sharedApiOpportunity: Add a reusable frame/variant surface on DiffViewer so alternate mock framing is part of the API instead of external wrapper markup.

recurringWrappers.pattern: Chat/editor metadata packaged through surrounding card structure
recurringWrappers.sharedApiOpportunity: Expose richer file metadata regions and compact framing on CodeEditor so chat/tool contexts do not depend on bespoke surrounding structure for hierarchy.

recurringWrappers.pattern: Docs embeds rely on outside layout context to tone down code chrome
recurringWrappers.sharedApiOpportunity: Add embedded framing controls to CodeBlock and DiffViewer so docs usage is first-class.

risks: Diff layout changes can regress narrow-width behavior if the asymmetric mode is not explicit in CSS.
New frame variants can drift visually between vellum and void themes if the styles are hard-coded instead of token-driven.
Mockup consumers may still carry styling assumptions after wrapper removal and need careful spot checks.

runtimeContexts.name: shared code surfaces
runtimeContexts.reason: These files define and validate the exported CodeBlock, CodeEditor, and DiffViewer primitives.

runtimeContexts.name: chat embedded editor
runtimeContexts.reason: Chat tool cards embed metadata-heavy editors inside a denser memo layout and need quieter framing plus richer file metadata.

runtimeContexts.name: docs embedded code
runtimeContexts.reason: Docs pages embed code surfaces inline with article typography and margin systems, so the component needs an embedded treatment rather than relying on outside wrappers.

runtimeContexts.name: mock diff framing
runtimeContexts.reason: The Seraph mock wraps DiffViewer in a bespoke parchment frame, which signals a recurring alternate-frame requirement the shared API should absorb.

storyCoverage.reason: Acceptance explicitly requires direct story coverage for rich file metadata.
Docs/chat/mock embed states should be inspectable without relying on MockupPreviews.
The recurring Seraph/docs wrapper should become a direct reusable story.
This is the highest-risk API expansion area and needs direct visual coverage.
Acceptance explicitly calls for empty states.
storyCoverage.state: empty editor and empty diff states

targetStateMatrix.embedded: must sit inside article and card compositions without extra wrapper shells
targetStateMatrix.emptyState: quiet placeholder that still feels intentional inside prose
targetStateMatrix.metadata: title, meta, optional fact rail, compact footer

targetStateMatrix.embedded: must read cleanly inside dense chat memo and tool-card layouts
targetStateMatrix.emptyState: empty file/editor state with preserved header and file rail
targetStateMatrix.framing: editor chrome must support compact and embedded variants
targetStateMatrix.metadata: filename, fileMeta, status, facts, footer, additional file metadata rows

targetStateMatrix.framing: standard panel frame with tabs and head
targetStateMatrix.metadata: file meta, note, per-side labels, summary counts

targetStateMatrix.diffShape: supports asymmetric, added-only, removed-only, and side-hidden views
targetStateMatrix.embedded: must render inside docs figures and mock cards directly
targetStateMatrix.emptyState: embedded empty state must inherit host tone
targetStateMatrix.framing: alternate framed treatment matching docs/mock language without external wrapper divs
targetStateMatrix.metadata: same metadata richness as default plus embedded title/meta treatment

targetStateMatrix.diffShape: before-only, after-only, and intentionally hidden opposite side
targetStateMatrix.framing: single-column or emphasis-first layout when only one side exists
targetStateMatrix.metadata: side labels and file metadata remain readable without dead empty space

testCoverage.reason: Richer metadata rails are easy to regress structurally.
testCoverage.state: metadata-heavy editor framing and file metadata regions

testCoverage.reason: Frame variants drive theme alignment and wrapper removal.
Asymmetric layouts are the highest-risk rendering change.
testCoverage.state: one-sided diff rendering without redundant opposite column

testCoverage.reason: The current implementation only lightly covers placeholders and needs stronger permutation checks.
